reasons: The dog appears to have normal, healthy muscle mass. The temporal muscles look full, not wasted. The spine is not prominent or razor-backed. The shoulders (scapulae) do not appear sharp or winged. The hindquarters maintain a rounded, muscular profile rather than a flat or concave appearance indicative of severe wasting. There are no obvious signs of significant muscle loss across the typical areas (head, spine, shoulders, hips/hind limbs) that would suggest an increased risk for chronic disease, cancer, or severe malnutrition.
